page: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-384 is a graphics-optimized server instance configured with 384 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture. The hardware profile includes 1440.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per vCPU. Acceleration is provided by eight integrated NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPUs. This instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

As a high-density GPU platform, this server is engineered for massive parallel processing workloads. The combination of 384 dedicated vCPUs and eight professional-grade GPUs provides substantial compute density. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the consolidation of eight GPUs and a high vCPU count into a single instance reduces the overhead of managing multi-node clusters for large-scale rendering or visualization tasks, though it represents a significant resource commitment.

The g4-standard-384 is designed for workloads requiring intensive graphical computation and parallel processing. Suitable use cases include large-scale 3D rendering, computer-aided design pipelines, video encoding, and GPU-accelerated simulation workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-384 is a graphics-optimized server instance designed for high-density parallel computing. It features 384 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture and 1440.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 3.75 GB of RAM per core. The primary hardware accelerator configuration consists of eight NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPUs, making it highly capable for graphics-intensive tasks. The instance does not feature local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, this server offers a high-density resource profile that consolidates massive GPU and CPU resources, minimizing the need for multi-node cluster management. It is suited for workloads such as large-scale 3D rendering, complex simulations, and GPU-accelerated visualization pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-384 is a graphics-optimized server featuring 384 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 1440.0 GB of RAM, and eight NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPUs for parallel computing workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-384 features 384 dedicated vCPUs, 1440.0 GB RAM, and eight NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPUs for graphics workloads.
tagline: A high-density graphics-optimized platform featuring eight professional GPUs and three hundred eighty-four dedicated virtual cores for parallel processing.
bullet_points: Eight integrated NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPUs for hardware acceleration.
384 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for high-performance host processing.
1440.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core.
No local storage included, requiring external storage attachment.
Designed for large-scale 3D rendering and GPU-accelerated simulations.
